_comment: Canonical lookup: Joshua's Mammotion-app area names â†’ HA hash. HA's auto-generated friendly_names ('Area 1'-'Area 8') do NOT match Joshua's app names. Use the 'by_app_name' map below when issuing mammotion.start_mow.
_authoritative_source: Joshua Montgomery visual identification via Google Earth (c:/temp/zones.kml)

service_call_template._comment: mammotion.start_mow expects the entity_id form of the area switch in the 'areas' list (e.g. 'switch.luba2_awd_1_area_3291496386505131361' or HA-generated short forms like 'switch.luba2_awd_1_area_area_10'). The raw hash form returns HTTP 400. Use by_app_name[X].ha_switch_entity, NOT by_app_name[X].hash. Confirmed 2026-05-13 by mycroft-container cross-validation.

operational_notes.warning: Always issue cancel_job AFTER lawn_mower.dock â€” recall sends mower home but leaves job in 'paused' state. cancel_job transitions mower to MODE_READY + docked.

drift_detection._why: Mammotion area hashes are content-derived from polygon vertices. Border-edit (for growth/erosion/landscape change) → new hash. This mapping WILL go stale when Joshua adjusts borders.
drift_detection._detection: Before issuing area-targeted command, pull current GeoJSON via mammotion.get_geojson. For each by_app_name entry, verify the stored hash still appears in the GeoJSON. If any hash missing → drift detected.
_recovery_strategy: 1. Pull fresh GeoJSON via HA service mammotion.get_geojson(entity_id=lawn_mower.luba2_awd_1).
2. For each area in by_app_name, look up stored centroid_lat_lon.
3. For each polygon in fresh GeoJSON, compute its centroid.
4. Match by closest centroid (haversine or simple lat/lon distance) within ~20m tolerance.
5. If unambiguous match found, update by_app_name[X].hash + by_hash + centroid_lat_lon in this file + commit + push.
6. If ambiguous OR no match within tolerance, ASK Joshua to re-identify via regenerated KML.
drift_detection._kml_regeneration_command: See ~/projects/mower-recovery-pm/scripts/regenerate-mapping-kml.sh (TODO) OR re-run the get_geojson-to-KML python flow used 2026-05-12.
drift_detection._check_cadence_recommendation: Before every area-targeted command (cheap — one HA service call + dict lookup) OR daily cron + Redis alert on drift.
